description: This plot presents the average dynamic recrystallization grain size of low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel after 60% hot compression, as a function of temperature (ranging from 900 °C to 1150 °C) under four different strain rates: 0.01 s⁻¹ (black line with square markers), 0.1 s⁻¹ (red line with circle markers), 1 s⁻¹ (blue line with triangle markers), and 10 s⁻¹ (purple line with star markers). The y-axis represents grain size in μm, while the x-axis is deformation temperature in °C. For all strain rates, the DRX grain size monotonically increases with rising temperature. At the same temperature, lower strain rate leads to larger grain size: at 1150 °C and 0.01 s⁻¹, grain size exceeds 70 μm, while it is only around 5 μm at 900 °C and 10 s⁻¹. The figure confirms that DRX grain size of the studied steel is positively correlated with deformation temperature and negatively correlated with strain rate, supporting the establishment of the grain size prediction model for hot processing optimization of this steel.
